production_fidelity_environment.engine: Playwright headless Chromium v1223 (Chrome Headless Shell 148.0.7778.96) + Node http.createServer hosting output/integration/ on 127.0.0.1:8088-8090 + live network fetches against https://statsapi.mlb.com

components.name: browser_runtime
components.source: Playwright Chromium installed at /sessions/adoring-ecstatic-volta/.cache/ms-playwright/chromium_headless_shell-1223 — real V8, real DOM, real ES module loader, real fetch implementation

components.name: statsapi_mlb_com
components.cors_observed: Fetches succeeded from a non-mlb.com origin (127.0.0.1) in a real browser — CORS is effectively permissive, confirming the canonical-evidence assumption in TD-IP-2's cors_verification

components.name: github_pages_deployment_url
components.source: https://mondrianaire.github.io/auto-builder/runs/mlb-daily-dashboard/output/final/ — URL pattern matches the auto-builder repo's documented GitHub Pages target. Live URL probe deferred to Orchestrator post-deploy per CV.LIVE notes in edge-case-testing test-plan.

first_contact_results.description: Page loads successfully, no blank screen, no console-fatal errors
first_contact_results.evidence: HTTP 200 from local server; page.goto resolved; 0 console errors during full load+render cycle

first_contact_results.description: Within a few seconds, dashboard renders actual current MLB data
first_contact_results.evidence: All three panels transitioned out of 'Loading...' state within ~3s of DOMContentLoaded; standings show TB 34-17, NYY 32-22, CLE 32-24 etc. — real 2026 mid-season values

first_contact_results.description: All three focus areas visible: weekly trends, upcoming games, overall rankings
first_contact_results.evidence: #rankings-panel (16090 chars rendered), #trends-panel (21021 chars rendered, 30 trend rows), #upcoming-panel (59807 chars rendered, 105 game rows) — all populated with real data, none in loading state

All 30 expected team abbreviations (LAA, ARI, BAL, BOS, CHC, CIN, CLE, COL, DET, HOU, KC, LAD, WSH, NYM, ATH, PIT, SD, SEA, SF, STL, TB, TEX, TOR, MIN, PHI, ATL, CWS, MIA, NYY, MIL) found in DOM via tokenized scan. Note: Arizona renders as 'AZ' in standings/trends panels (API-returned abbreviation) and as 'ARI' in upcoming panel (from teams.js metadata) — same team, two abbreviations. Both are valid MLB designators; this does not break the requirement, but see concerns.
#last-updated text reads '2026-05-26 06:15' (build date). Upcoming games show dates TUE MAY 26, WED MAY 27, THU MAY 28 — all within [today, today+7]. Standings reflect mid-season 2026 W-L records.

first_contact_results.description: Page hosted at publicly reachable GitHub Pages URL and URL is delivered
first_contact_results.evidence: Deployment target https://mondrianaire.github.io/auto-builder/runs/mlb-daily-dashboard/output/final/ is declared in manifest.json and matches the documented auto-builder Pages pattern. Per the briefing's deferral note, live URL test is for Orchestrator post-commit; the artifact is verified ready-to-deploy.

first_contact_results.description: Next-day return visit shows refreshed data
first_contact_results.evidence: Browser page.reload() triggered all 4 statsapi.mlb.com calls again (no localStorage cache check, no service worker). Source review confirms app.js always re-runs init() on DOMContentLoaded; no persistence layer; no stale-cache lock-in.

prompt_named_verb_result.verb_from_prompt: show a daily dashboard of MLB teams covering weekly trends, upcoming games, and overall rankings
prompt_named_verb_result.scenario_executed: Playwright Chromium loaded http://127.0.0.1:8088/index.html, waited for live statsapi.mlb.com data, then inspected the DOM
prompt_named_verb_result.expected_result: Page contains (a) rankings/standings section listing all 30 MLB teams with W-L records, (b) weekly-trends section with last-7-days performance for each team, (c) upcoming-games section listing games in next 7 days, AND no error banner

observed_result.rankings_panel: 8 division-block elements (6 divisions + 2 wild-card blocks); 36 team rows in division tables (30 MLB teams + wildcard repeats); each row shows team abbr + W + L + PCT + GB
observed_result.trends_panel: 30 trend-row elements rendered; each shows abbreviation, sparkline SVG, W-L pair, run differential, and streak (e.g., 'AZ 5-1 +16 W3')
observed_result.upcoming_panel: 105 upcoming-game rows grouped by day (TUE MAY 26 through ~JUN 2); each row shows away @ home with start time

assumption_checks.label: Single web page
assumption_checks.evidence: output/integration/ contains exactly one HTML entry (index.html); no router/multi-page setup

assumption_checks.label: Team-level stats only
assumption_checks.evidence: No /people/ or /roster/ endpoint calls observed; no player-row DOM elements

assumption_checks.label: Live fetch on each page load
assumption_checks.evidence: FC.7 — reload triggered 4 fresh statsapi.mlb.com calls; no localStorage caching

assumption_checks.label: Last 7 days W-L + run diff
assumption_checks.evidence: trends-engine.js computes 7-day window; fetchRecentResults(7) called with start=today-6, end=today; trends-panel rows display last-N W-L + run diff

assumption_checks.label: Today + next 7 days
assumption_checks.evidence: fetchSchedule called with startDate=2026-05-26, endDate=2026-06-02 (today + 7); rendered dates span MAY 26 through JUN 2

assumption_checks.label: Division standings
assumption_checks.evidence: Six division-block elements rendered: AL East, AL Central, AL West, NL East, NL Central, NL West; plus 2 wild-card blocks

assumption_checks.label: Read-only, no accounts
assumption_checks.evidence: No login/auth code; no localStorage writes; no preference UI

assumption_checks.label: Windows host, browser-agnostic UI
assumption_checks.evidence: All JS uses forward-slash imports; no shebangs; vanilla CSS + ES modules portable across OSes

assumption_checks.label: Mid-season, live data expected
assumption_checks.evidence: Live statsapi response delivered non-empty standings (30 teams) and non-empty schedule (105 games over 7-day window) — confirms 2026 season is live

out_of_scope_checks.evidence: data-client uses currentSeason() = new Date().getFullYear(); no historical season iteration
No manifest.json for PWA, no service-worker.js, no register-service-worker
Only static files (.html/.css/.js); no Express/Fastify/serverless functions
No image files; no mlbstatic.com references; team identity via abbreviation + color swatch only
All API calls use sportId=1 (MLB only)

inflection_point_checks.evidence: All fetch URLs target https://statsapi.mlb.com/api/v1/; 4 distinct endpoints exercised live

inflection_point_checks.chosen_branch: Client-side fetch on every page load
inflection_point_checks.evidence: FC.7 confirms fresh fetches on every load; no snapshot JSON committed
No player endpoints called; team-row UI only
fetchRecentResults(7) uses daysAgoISO(6) → today inclusive window
fetchSchedule(today, today+7); upcoming-panel rendered dates span MAY 26 through JUN 2
computeRankings emits divisionStandings with 6 keys + wildCard with 2 keys; renderRankings outputs 6 division blocks + 2 wild-card blocks (AL + NL)
Stubbed empty schedule scenario: #upcoming-panel shows 'No games scheduled in the next 7 days.'
30 CSS --team-* custom properties defined; .team-swatch elements use color vars; no tags for logos
renderSparkline builds <svg><polyline> inline; no chart.js/d3/echarts/plotly/highcharts references
index.html has <script type="module" src="app.js">; all .js files use export/import
No tailwind/bootstrap/bulma references; styles.css is hand-authored with CSS custom properties

edge_case_testing_report_review.comment: Edge-case-testing ran 8 scenarios under Node-static-fidelity (no Playwright at that layer). The 6 assertions it deferred to CV (live browser + live data scenarios) have all been exercised here and pass.

static_inspection_summary.fetch_outside_data_client: Zero fetch() calls outside data-client.js (verified via grep across all JS files)
static_inspection_summary.statsapi_url_outside_data_client: One textual reference in index.html footer link (<a href="https://statsapi.mlb.com/api/v1/">) — informational link, not a fetch. Strictly S1.A4 says 'fetch() and to statsapi.mlb.com URLs' which would catch this, but the assertion is verifier:critic_final_sweep not cv_artifact_exercise; flagged for awareness only.
static_inspection_summary.no_external_cdn_runtime_deps: Zero matches for cdn./unpkg/jsdelivr/cdnjs/googleapis.com in any file
static_inspection_summary.es_module_script_present: <script type="module" src="app.js"> on line 59 of index.html
static_inspection_summary.no_inline_script_blocks: Zero <script>...</script> blocks in index.html (only the module tag)

summary.headline: MLB Daily Dashboard delivers the prompt's intent under production fidelity. Real Chromium loaded the integrated artifact, real statsapi.mlb.com calls returned real 2026 mid-season data, all three focus areas (rankings, trends, upcoming) rendered correctly, all 30 MLB teams present, no console errors, error path and empty-state path both verified.
summary.fc_results: FC.1 pass | FC.2 pass | FC.3 pass | FC.4 pass | FC.5 pass | FC.6 pass_pending_deploy (URL pattern verified, live probe deferred to Orchestrator) | FC.7 pass
summary.pnv_result: pass (real browser, real network, all three focus areas + 30 teams rendered, no error banner)
concerns: Arizona Diamondbacks: rendered as 'AZ' in standings/trends panels (API teamAbbreviation field) but as 'ARI' in upcoming panel (teams.js metadata). Single team displays under two abbreviations on the same page. Functional and not a delivery failure — all 30 teams are clearly present — but a polish nit. Not a Sev 0 trivial fix (would require either patching teams.js mapping to 'AZ' or normalizing the API response in data-client.js, both >5 lines). Recommend Orchestrator capture as a known cosmetic issue for the wrap-up or post-ratify backlog.
FC.6 live URL not probed in CV — deferred to Orchestrator post-commit per briefing. Artifact is verified ready-to-deploy; the only remaining step is the commit-build push.
summary.fidelity_environment_used: Option A (preferred) — Playwright headless Chromium + live statsapi.mlb.com network
